channel.title: Edgar's Personal Wiki
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Edgar's Personal Wiki

item.title: Edgar's Personal Wiki
item.description: Edgar’s Personal Wiki Public notebook This is the public edge of a private working wiki: source-backed notes about software, systems, books, institutions, and the moments where fiction explains reality better than reality explains itself.

item.title: ai-era-engineering-apprenticeship
item.description: AI-Era Engineering Apprenticeship AI-era engineering apprenticeship is the user’s emerging question about how people develop judgment when AI agents absorb more of the small execution work that previously trained juniors and mid-level engineers.

item.title: choosing-what-to-work-on
item.description: Choosing What to Work On Choosing what to work on is the judgment of selecting problems whose importance, evidence, timing, and fit justify sustained attention.

item.title: developer-tools
item.description: Developer Tools Developer tools, in Michael Bolin’s Peterman Pod episode, are high-leverage engineering systems because they change how quickly and effectively other engineers can build.

item.title: engineering-career-growth
item.description: Engineering Career Growth Engineering career growth, in the currently ingested Peterman Pod sources, is less about doing more of the same work and more about matching behavior to the next level’s expectations.

item.title: recursive-self-improvement
item.description: Recursive Self-Improvement Recursive self-improvement is the threshold where an AI system can autonomously design and develop its own successor.

item.title: when-ai-builds-itself
item.description: When AI Builds Itself When AI builds itself is an Anthropic Institute article about Recursive Self-Improvement.

item.title: anthropic-institute
item.description: The Anthropic Institute The Anthropic Institute is currently represented in this wiki by one article about recursive self-improvement and the role of AI systems in accelerating AI development.

item.title: bell-labs-research-culture
item.description: Bell Labs Research Culture Bell Labs research culture, in Bjarne Stroustrup’s Peterman Pod episode, is a model of practical engineering research built around talent density, autonomy, and concise accountability.

item.title: big-tech-promotion-culture
item.description: Big-Tech Promotion Culture Big-tech promotion culture, in the currently ingested Peterman Pod sources, is the incentive system that turns career advancement into a distinct game with its own tactics, timing, narratives, and failure modes.
